evals.prompt: Assess this repository as a new contributor would. Tell me what is healthy, what is unclear or risky, and give me the five highest-value improvements in priority order. Apart from the assessment report, do not change any files.
evals.expected_output: A read-only, evidence-backed assessment that follows repository instructions, maps the project, reports strengths and risks, and gives five concrete prioritized improvements with validation limits. Each numbered finding maps to the improvement with the same number and exact title. The complete report is saved as a Markdown artifact and its path is identified in the response.

evals.prompt: We're considering putting this project into production next quarter. Review the current directory for production readiness, focusing on testing, security, deployment, observability, and operational documentation. Flag anything you could not verify and don't install dependencies or contact external services.
evals.expected_output: A production-readiness assessment scoped to relevant evidence, with explicit uncertainties, safe validation, and prioritized recommendations rather than a generic checklist. Each numbered finding maps to the improvement with the same number and exact title. The complete report is saved as a Markdown artifact and its path is identified in the response.

evals.prompt: Take a quick look around this small prototype and suggest what I should improve next. Keep the advice proportional—I want useful next steps, not enterprise ceremony—and cite the files that led you to each conclusion.
evals.expected_output: A concise assessment calibrated to a prototype, citing project evidence, preserving strengths, and recommending proportionate next steps without unnecessary process overhead. Each numbered finding maps to the improvement with the same number and exact title. The complete report is saved as a Markdown artifact and its path is identified in the response.
